audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Sodom hospitality violation, yada as attempted sexual violence, Lot's compromised offer, divine rescue, sulfur/fire judgment, Lot's wife, cave episode, and Moab/Ammon origins. Final source-critical review may refine idiom and ancient legal/social context.

verses.restored: The two messengers came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gate of Sodom. Lot saw them, rose to meet them, and bowed with his face to the earth.

familiar.text: And there came two angels to Sodom at even; and Lot sat in the gate of Sodom...

notes.title: Gate of Sodom
notes.body: Sitting in the gate suggests civic presence or status. Lot is now integrated into Sodom’s public life.

verses.restored: He said, Look now, my lords, please turn aside to your servant’s house, spend the night, wash your feet, and rise early and go on your way. They said, No, for we will spend the night in the square.

familiar.text: And he said, Behold now, my lords, turn in, I pray you, into your servant's house...

notes.title: Hospitality pressure
notes.body: Lot urges private shelter because the city is unsafe.

verses.restored: He pressed them strongly, and they turned aside to him and came into his house. He made a feast for them and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.

familiar.text: And he pressed upon them greatly; and they turned in unto him...

verses.restored: Before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, surrounded the house, from young to old, all the people from every quarter.

familiar.text: But before they lay down, the men of the city, even the men of Sodom, compassed the house round...

notes.title: All the people
notes.body: The mob is described broadly across age and quarter, portraying communal corruption rather than one individual sin.

verses.restored: They called to Lot and said to him, Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, so that we may know them.

familiar.text: And they called unto Lot... bring them out unto us, that we may know them.

notes.title: Know them
notes.body: Yada can mean know sexually. In this context of mob threat, it means attempted sexual violence and domination, not loving relationship.

verses.restored: Lot went out to them at the entrance and shut the door behind him.

familiar.text: And Lot went out at the door unto them, and shut the door after him,

verses.restored: He said, Please, my brothers, do not do harm.

familiar.text: And said, I pray you, brethren, do not so wickedly.

notes.title: Do not do harm
notes.body: Ra'a here names the mob’s intended action as harm/evil.

verses.restored: Look now, I have two daughters who have not known a man. Please let me bring them out to you, and do to them what is good in your eyes. Only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shadow of my roof.

familiar.text: Behold now, I have two daughters which have not known man; let me, I pray you, bring them out unto you...

notes.title: Lot’s horrifying offer
notes.body: Lot attempts to protect guests by offering his daughters. The text reports this without approving it. It exposes patriarchal hospitality distorted by violence.

notes.title: Good in your eyes
notes.body: The phrase is morally chilling here; what seems good in the mob’s eyes is not good.

verses.restored: They said, Stand back. And they said, This one came to sojourn, and now he would surely judge? Now we will do more harm to you than to them. They pressed hard against the man, against Lot, and came near to break the door.

familiar.text: And they said, Stand back. And they said again, This one fellow came in to sojourn...

notes.title: Sojourner judged by city
notes.body: The mob rejects Lot as an outsider who dares to judge them, showing Sodom’s hostility to the vulnerable sojourner.

verses.restored: But the men stretched out their hands, brought Lot into the house with them, and shut the door.

familiar.text: But the men put forth their hand, and pulled Lot into the house to them, and shut to the door.

verses.restored: They struck the men who were at the entrance of the house, from small to great, with blindness, so that they wore themselves out trying to find the entrance.

familiar.text: And they smote the men that were at the door of the house with blindness...

verses.restored: The men said to Lot, Who else do you have here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, and anyone you have in the city: bring them out of the place.

familiar.text: And the men said unto Lot, Hast thou here any besides? son in law, and thy sons...

verses.restored: For we are about to ruin this place, because their outcry has become great before the face of the Eternal, and the Eternal has sent us to ruin it.

familiar.text: For we will destroy this place, because the cry of them is waxen great before the face of the LORD...

notes.title: Outcry
notes.body: The judgment is tied to outcry, the cry of victims or distress, not arbitrary anger.

verses.restored: Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law, who were taking his daughters, and said, Rise, go out from this place, for the Eternal is about to ruin the city. But he seemed like one joking in the eyes of his sons-in-law.

familiar.text: And Lot went out, and spake unto his sons in law... But he seemed as one that mocked...

notes.title: Seemed like joking
notes.body: The warning is not believed, showing the depth of disbelief inside impending judgment.

verses.restored: As dawn came up, the messengers urged Lot, saying, Rise, take your woman and your two daughters who are found here, lest you be swept away in the iniquity of the city.

familiar.text: And when the morning arose, then the angels hastened Lot...

verses.restored: But he lingered. The men seized his hand, the hand of his woman, and the hands of his two daughters, because the Eternal had compassion on him. They brought him out and set him outside the city.

familiar.text: And while he lingered, the men laid hold upon his hand... the LORD being merciful unto him...

notes.title: Compassion in forceful rescue
notes.body: Lot lingers, and the messengers physically remove the family. The text names this as compassion.

verses.restored: When they had brought them outside, one said, Escape for your life. Do not look behind you, and do not stop anywhere in the plain. Escape to the mountain, lest you be swept away.

familiar.text: Escape for thy life; look not behind thee, neither stay thou in all the plain...

verses.restored: Lot said to them, Please, no, my lord.

familiar.text: And Lot said unto them, Oh, not so, my Lord:

verses.restored: Look, your servant has found favor in your eyes, and you have made your steadfast kindness great, which you have done for me by keeping my life alive. But I cannot escape to the mountain, lest the harm cling to me and I die.

familiar.text: Behold now, thy servant hath found grace in thy sight...

verses.restored: Look, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is small. Please let me escape there—is it not small?—and my life shall live.

familiar.text: Behold now, this city is near to flee unto, and it is a little one...

verses.restored: He said to him, Look, I have lifted your face even in this matter, not to overturn the city of which you have spoken.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, See, I have accepted thee concerning this thing also...

notes.title: Lifted your face
notes.body: An idiom for granting favor or accepting a request.

verses.restored: Hurry, escape there, for I cannot do anything until you arrive there. Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.

familiar.text: Haste thee, escape thither; for I cannot do any thing till thou be come thither...

notes.title: Zoar
notes.body: Zoar is linked with smallness, matching Lot’s plea for a small city.

verses.restored: The sun had gone out over the earth when Lot came to Zoar.

familiar.text: The sun was risen upon the earth when Lot entered into Zoar.

verses.restored: Then the Eternal rained upon Sodom and Gomorrah sulfur and fire from the Eternal out of the heavens.

familiar.text: Then the LORD rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire...

notes.title: Sulfur and fire
notes.body: The judgment language is severe. The text gives no permission to detach it from the preceding outcry and violence.

verses.restored: He overturned those cities, the whole plain, all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew from the ground.

familiar.text: And he overthrew those cities, and all the plain, and all the inhabitants...

verses.restored: But his woman looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.

familiar.text: But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.

notes.title: Looked back
notes.body: The text does not explain her inner motive. It simply reports the act against the warning and the result.

verses.restored: Abraham rose early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the Eternal.

familiar.text: And Abraham gat up early in the morning to the place where he stood before the LORD:

verses.restored: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and toward all the face of the land of the plain, and he saw, and look: smoke of the land was rising like smoke of a furnace.

familiar.text: And he looked toward Sodom and Gomorrah... and beheld, and, lo, the smoke of the country went up...

verses.restored: When the Divine ruined the cities of the plain, the Divine remembered Abraham and sent Lot out from the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities where Lot had dwelled.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when God destroyed the cities of the plain, that God remembered Abraham...

notes.title: Remembered Abraham
notes.body: Lot’s rescue is linked to divine remembering of Abraham, connecting intercession and mercy.

verses.restored: Lot went up from Zoar and dwelled in the mountain, with his two daughters, for he was afraid to dwell in Zoar. He dwelled in a cave, he and his two daughters.

familiar.text: And Lot went up out of Zoar, and dwelt in the mountain...

verses.restored: The firstborn said to the younger, Our father is old, and there is no man on the earth to come in to us according to the way of all the earth.

familiar.text: And the firstborn said unto the younger, Our father is old, and there is not a man in the earth...

notes.title: Perceived end of world
notes.body: The daughters act from isolation and a perception that no future remains.

verses.restored: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and let us lie with him, so that we may preserve seed from our father.

familiar.text: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him...

notes.title: Preserve seed
notes.body: The daughters’ plan is framed as preserving lineage. The text is disturbing and should not be sanitized.

verses.restored: They made their father drink wine that night. The firstborn came in and lay with her father, and he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.

familiar.text: And they made their father drink wine that night: and the firstborn went in, and lay with her father...

notes.title: He did not know
notes.body: Lot’s lack of awareness is emphasized. The scene reverses earlier themes of drunkenness, nakedness, and family violation.

verses.restored: The next day, the firstborn said to the younger, Look, I lay last night with my father. Let us make him drink wine tonight also, and you go in and lie with him, so that we may preserve seed from our father.

familiar.text: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the firstborn said unto the younger...

verses.restored: They made their father drink wine that night also. The younger rose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.

familiar.text: And they made their father drink wine that night also: and the younger arose, and lay with him...

verses.restored: So the two daughters of Lot conceived by their father.

familiar.text: Thus were both the daughters of Lot with child by their father.

verses.restored: The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moab. He is the father of Moab to this day.

familiar.text: And the firstborn bare a son, and called his name Moab...

notes.title: Moab
notes.body: Moab is explained through wordplay as from father, giving an origin story for the Moabites.

verses.restored: The younger also bore a son and called his name Ben-Ammi. He is the father of the sons of Ammon to this day.

familiar.text: And the younger, she also bare a son, and called his name Benammi...

notes.title: Ben-Ammi
notes.body: Ben-Ammi means son of my people/kin, giving an origin story for Ammon.
